No Poverty.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to eradicating poverty or its specific targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to ending hunger, food security, or nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to health, well-being, or related targets.

Quality Education.reason: The course is a university-level program (Target 4.3) focused on developing advanced business communication, writing, research, and public speaking skills, directly preparing students for employment and entrepreneurship (Target 4.4).
evidence: Master essential writing skills for business purposes
Acquire research and analytical writing skills

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to energy access or clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course teaches students to develop business plans to attract investment and promote business concepts, directly fostering entrepreneurship and skills for decent work (Target 8.3).
evidence: complete a business report (BR) or a business plan (BP)
attract investment, or promote a business concept
Master essential writing skills for business purposes

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course involves developing business plans and conducting market/industry research to promote new business concepts, directly supporting innovation and enterprise growth (Targets 9.3, 9.5, 9.b).
evidence: complete a business report (BR) or a business plan (BP)

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to reducing inequalities based on income, age, sex, disability, race, ethnicity, or other status.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to sustainable urbanization, housing, or public spaces.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core Competence B explicitly focuses on students understanding and identifying 'ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues,' directly supporting the adoption of sustainable practices (Target 12.6).
evidence: B. 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題
Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, there is no specific evidence linking the course content to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or awareness.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or deforestation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's mention of 'Crisis Management' and 'ethics, social responsibility' offers a very weak, inferred link to promoting accountable institutions (Target 16.6) through responsible business practices.
evidence: 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course explicitly involves an international partnership with USC and an objective for students to 'Cooperate and collaborate internationally,' directly supporting global partnerships (Targets 17.6, 17.16).
evidence: a micro-course offered by the University of South California (USC)
Cooperate and collaborate internationally to complete a business communication project
